gaps: OAuth write operations not supported (out of scope for the trimmed read-only build)
Analytics, revenue, retention require YouTube Analytics API + OAuth, not included
captions.download not supported (use timedtext transcript fetcher instead)
Transcripts may fail with RequestBlocked from cloud IPs; residential IP recommended
`videos related` is heuristic (topic + channel + tag overlap), not Google's real recommendation engine

novel_features.name: Bulk search from stdin
novel_features.description: Take a list of search terms from stdin or args, return top-N YouTube videos per term in one JSON document with titles, channels, embed URLs, and thumbnails.
novel_features.rationale: No existing CLI or MCP takes a list of search terms. Drops directly into a photo-keywords -> videos -> blog-post pipeline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you have N search terms from an upstream pipeline (image labels, photo tags, scraped keywords), reach for this instead of looping single searches.

novel_features.name: Transcript fetcher
novel_features.description: Fetch the spoken-content transcript of a YouTube video using the timedtext endpoint. Works for auto-generated and manual captions on any public video. Caches into the local store.
novel_features.rationale: The YouTube Data API exposes caption metadata but not text (captions.download requires OAuth as owner). The timedtext endpoint is the standard workaround and needs no API key.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Read the transcript before deciding whether a candidate video actually fits the topic of the blog post or photo.

novel_features.name: Embed snippet generator
novel_features.description: Print embed HTML, iframe, or markdown-style embed for a video ID. Direct copy-paste into a blog draft.
novel_features.rationale: Every blog platform expects a slightly different embed shape; no competing CLI emits a ready-to-paste embed snippet.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Once you've picked a video for the blog, get the embed snippet without remembering the exact iframe URL pattern.

novel_features.name: Topic-similarity related videos
novel_features.description: Find videos related to a target video using topicDetails + same-channel + tag overlap from the local store. Best-effort replacement for the deprecated relatedToVideoId parameter.
novel_features.rationale: YouTube deprecated relatedToVideoId in June 2023; no API replacement exists. Only possible because we sync topicDetails and tags into the local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When one candidate video is on-topic but you want more like it, this is the working stand-in for the deprecated parameter.

novel_features.name: Top comments by likes
novel_features.description: Fetch top comments on a video, ranked locally by likeCount. Pulls up to 5 pages from commentThreads.list and sorts so most-liked floats regardless of API order.
novel_features.rationale: commentThreads.list returns by relevance or time, not by likeCount. Local sort surfaces audience-validated comments that the default API ordering buries.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to gauge whether a video is actually well-received before embedding, or to surface viewer-supplied context the description doesn't mention.

novel_features.name: Channel uploads in one call
novel_features.description: List a channel's most recent uploads in one call. Resolves @handle or channelId, walks the auto-generated uploads playlist, returns video IDs + titles + publish times.
novel_features.rationale: The standard YouTube workflow requires two calls (channels.list -> contentDetails.relatedPlaylists.uploads, then playlistItems.list). One command collapses that.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces the manual two-step lookup. Pairs naturally with --for-handle workflows.

novel_features_built.name: Bulk search from stdin
novel_features_built.description: Take a list of search terms from stdin or args, return top-N YouTube videos per term in one JSON document with titles, channels, embed URLs, and thumbnails.

narrative.headline: Search YouTube in bulk, grab transcripts, get embed snippets — for the photo-keywords-to-blog-post workflow.
narrative.value_prop: A trimmed read-only YouTube Data API v3 CLI built for local research workflows. Bulk search from stdin, transcript fetching via timedtext (no OAuth needed), embed snippet generation, and a heuristic-based `videos related` that still works despite the 2023 deprecation.
narrative.auth_narrative: API-key only — set `YOUTUBE_API_KEY` and you're done. Read-only public-data operations only (10,000 quota units/day default). Write operations are not configured; this CLI is for discovery and research, not channel management.

quickstart.comment: Verify the API key and reachability.
Bulk search from positional args — the photo-keywords → videos shape (use --stdin to read from a file/pipe instead).
Fetch the transcript to verify topic relevance before picking the video.
Get a markdown embed snippet for the blog draft.
Find more candidates similar to a video you've already picked.

troubleshoots.fix: Run from a residential IP (your laptop). YouTube blocks AWS/GCP/Azure IPs from the timedtext endpoint.

troubleshoots.symptom: `videos related` returns no results for a fresh video ID
troubleshoots.fix: Run `youtube-pp-cli youtube videos-list --id <id> --parts topicDetails,snippet` first to populate topic/tag data in the local store, then retry.

Daily quota (10,000 units) is exhausted. search.list costs 100 units per call, videos.list costs 1. Switch to cached results via `youtube-pp-cli sql \"SELECT * FROM search_results WHERE query='...'\"`.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when you have search terms (from photo tags, image labels, or notes) and want relevant YouTube videos back — with transcripts to verify relevance and embed snippets to drop into a blog draft. It's read-only and local; not a webapp backend, not a write tool.

recipes.title: Photo keywords to candidate videos
recipes.explanation: Reads keywords (one per line), searches each, returns webapp-ready embed-URL JSON. Use --select with dotted paths to keep the output tight.

recipes.title: Verify a candidate via transcript
recipes.explanation: Pull the first 2KB of the transcript to confirm the video is actually about the topic before adding it to the blog.

recipes.title: Get a markdown embed for the draft
recipes.explanation: Drops a ready-to-paste markdown video reference into your editor buffer.

recipes.title: Find more candidates like a picked one
recipes.explanation: Heuristic-based replacement for the deprecated relatedToVideoId. Uses topic + channel + tag overlap from synced local data.

recipes.title: Two-step bulk research: search then transcript
recipes.explanation: Top results JSON. Pipe `.results[*].videoId` through jq + xargs into `youtube-pp-cli youtube videos-transcript {}` to extract transcripts for each.
